title: France faces new heatwave with temperatures reaching 40°C
subhed: Heat swept across much of the country on Monday, with temperatures reaching up to 38°C and even 40°C in the southwest. The third major heatwave of the year is expected to be both intense and prolonged, likely lasting around 12 days.

US leads international concern after China test-fires missile into Pacific
subhed: Though a spokesperson for China's navy stated on Monday that the test launch was simply a 'routine arrangement' of military training, the US state department is concerned that this demonstrates China's growing capacity to strike the US mainland.

title: 'A despicable woman unworthy of her office': Kylian Mbappé responds to racist remarks from Paraguayan senator
subhed: The captain of the French national football team responded on Monday to a racist message posted by Celeste Amarilla. The Paraguayan politician's comments came in the wake of the Albirroja's defeat by Les Bleus in the round of 16 of the World Cup.

title: 2026 World Cup: Spain beat Portugal 1-0 to reach quarter-finals
subhed: The European champions will face the United States, the only one of the co-hosts remaining, or Belgium on Friday in Los Angeles.

title: Somaliland's high-risk quest for recognition
subhed: The self-proclaimed republic aspires to accede to the international stage. It finds itself at the center of a game of alliances, caught between Israeli, American and Emirati ambitions and threats from Yemen and neighboring Somalia.

title: Politicial scientist Alexandra de Hoop Scheffer: 'The United States is grappling with strategic saturation'
subhed: According to the president of the German Marshall Fund, the US, which is engaged on multiple fronts, is renegotiating the transatlantic contract unilaterally by pressuring its European allies to take responsibility for their own security. This 'burden shifting' challenges Europe to coordinate the organization of its defense industries.

title: 250 years of American independence: Trump praises US and attacks 'communists' at chaotic celebration
subhed: After over an hour's delay due to a heatwave and thunderstorms, Republican President Donald Trump, who made Independence Day into a celebration of his glory, gave a speech, under the rain, in which he claimed that 'America is back.'
